memberdef.type: std::function< double(VertId a, VertId b, VertId c)>
memberdef.definition: using MR::FillTriangleMetric = std::function<double( VertId a, VertId b, VertId c )>
memberdef.name: FillTriangleMetric

briefdescription.para: args: three vertices of candidate triangle

memberdef.type: std::function< double(VertId a, VertId b, VertId l, VertId r)>
memberdef.definition: using MR::FillEdgeMetric = std::function<double( VertId a, VertId b, VertId l, VertId r )>
memberdef.name: FillEdgeMetric

detaileddescription.para: args: a->b: candidate edge l: next(a->b) note that they are not connected in topology untill triangulation process ends r: prev(a->b) note that they are not connected in topology untill triangulation process ends

briefdescription.para: args: two metric weights to combine (usualy it is simple sum of them)

detaileddescription.para: Big value, but less then DBL_MAX, to be able to pass some bad triangulations instead of breaking it e10 - real metrics to have weight in triangulation, if it would be more than e15+ some metrics will be less than double precision

memberdef.argsstring: (Mesh &mesh, EdgeId a, EdgeId b, const StitchHolesParams &params={})
memberdef.name: buildCylinderBetweenTwoHoles

detaileddescription.para: Build cylindrical patch to fill space between two holes represented by one of their edges each, default metric: ComplexStitchMetric
Next picture show, how newly generated faces can be smoothed MR::positionVertsSmoothly MR::subdivideMesh Stitch with smooth

parameterdescription.para: EdgeId which represents 1st hole (should not have valid left FaceId)
EdgeId which represents 2nd hole (should not have valid left FaceId)

briefdescription.para: this version finds holes in the mesh by itself and returns false if they are not found

detaileddescription.para: Fills given hole represented by one of its edges (having no valid left face), uses fillHoleTrivially if cannot fill hole without multiple edges, default metric: CircumscribedFillMetric
Next picture show, how newly generated faces can be smoothed MR::positionVertsSmoothly MR::subdivideMesh Fill with smooth

parameterdescription.para: EdgeId which represents hole (should not have valid left FaceId)

briefdescription.para: fill all holes given by their representative edges in

detaileddescription.para:
returns true if given loop is a boundary of one hole in given mesh topology:
every edge in the loop does not have left face,
next/prev edges in the loop are related as follows: next = topology.prev( prev.sym() ) if the function returns true, then any edge from the loop passed to fillHole will fill the same hole
prepares the plan how to triangulate the face or hole to the left of (e) (not filling it immediately), several getHoleFillPlan can work in parallel

memberdef.argsstring: (const Mesh &mesh, const std::vector< EdgeId > &holeRepresentativeEdges, const FillHoleParams &params={})
memberdef.name: getHoleFillPlans

detaileddescription.para: prepares the plans how to triangulate the faces or holes, each given by a boundary edge (with filling target to the left), the plans are prepared in parallel with minimal memory allocation compared to manual calling of several getHoleFillPlan(), but it can inefficient when some holes are very complex
prepares the plan how to triangulate the planar face or planar hole to the left of (e) (not filling it immediately), several getPlanarHoleFillPlan can work in parallel

prepares the plans how to triangulate the planar faces or holes, each given by a boundary edge (with filling target to the left), the plans are prepared in parallel with minimal memory allocation compared to manual calling of several getPlanarHoleFillPlan(), but it can inefficient when some holes are very complex

briefdescription.para: quickly triangulates the face or hole to the left of (e) given the plan (quickly compared to fillHole function)
Triangulates face of hole in mesh trivially .

detaileddescription.para: Fills given hole represented by one of its edges (having no valid left face) by creating one new vertex in the centroid of boundary vertices and connecting new vertex with all boundary vertices.
Next picture show, how newly generated faces can be smoothed MR::positionVertsSmoothly MR::subdivideMesh Trivial fill with smooth

parameterdescription.para: EdgeId points on the face or hole to the left that will be triangulated

detaileddescription.para:
adds cylindrical extension of given hole represented by one of its edges (having no valid left face) by adding new vertices located in given plane and 2 * number_of_hole_edge triangles;
the edge of new hole opposite to input edge (a)

adds cylindrical extension of too all holes of the mesh by calling extendHole(...);
representative edges of one per every hole after extension

memberdef.argsstring: (Mesh &mesh, EdgeId a, std::function< Vector3f(const Vector3f &)> getVertPos, FaceBitSet *outNewFaces=nullptr)
memberdef.name: extendHole

detaileddescription.para:
adds extension of given hole represented by one of its edges (having no valid left face) by adding new vertices located at getVertPos( existing vertex position );
the edge of new hole opposite to input edge (a)

memberdef.argsstring: (Mesh &mesh, EdgeId a, Vector3f dir, float holeExtension, FaceBitSet *outNewFaces=nullptr)
memberdef.name: buildBottom

detaileddescription.para:
adds cylindrical extension of given hole represented by one of its edges (having no valid left face) by adding new vertices located in lowest point of the hole -dir*holeExtension and 2 * number_of_hole_edge triangles;
the edge of new hole opposite to input edge (a)

creates a band of degenerate triangles around given hole;
the edge of new hole opposite to input edge (a)

creates a bridge between two boundary edges a and b (both having no valid left face); bridge consists of one quadrangle in general (beware that it cannot be rendered) or of one triangle if a and b are neighboring edges on the boundary;
false if bridge cannot be created because otherwise multiple edges appear

creates a bridge between two boundary edges a and b (both having no valid left face); bridge consists of two triangles in general or of one triangle if a and b are neighboring edges on the boundary;
MakeBridgeResult evaluating to false if bridge cannot be created because otherwise multiple edges appear

memberdef.argsstring: (Mesh &mesh, EdgeId a, EdgeId b, float samplingStep, FaceBitSet *outNewFaces=nullptr)
memberdef.name: makeSmoothBridge

detaileddescription.para:
creates a bridge between two boundary edges a and b (both having no valid left face); bridge consists of strip of quadrangles (each consisting of two triangles) in general or of some triangles if a and b are neighboring edges on the boundary; the bridge is made as smooth as possible with small angles in between its links and on the boundary with existed triangles;
samplingStep
boundaries of the bridge will be subdivided until the distance between neighbor points becomes less than this distance
MakeBridgeResult evaluating to false if bridge cannot be created because otherwise multiple edges appear

creates a new bridge edge between origins of two boundary edges a and b (both having no valid left face);
invalid id if bridge cannot be created because otherwise multiple edges appear

briefdescription.para: given quadrangle face to the left of a, splits it in two triangles with new diagonal edge via dest(a)
Computes combined metric after filling a hole.

detaileddescription.para: This metric minimizes the sum of circumcircle radii for all triangles in the triangulation. It is rather fast to calculate, and it results in typically good triangulations.
Same as getCircumscribedFillMetric, but with extra penalty for the triangles having normals looking in the opposite side of plane containing left of (e).

Similar to getPlaneFillMetric with extra penalty for the triangles having normals looking in the opposite side of plane containing left of (e), but the metric minimizes the sum of circumcircle radius times aspect ratio for all triangles in the triangulation.

This metric minimizes the sum of triangleMetric for all triangles in the triangulation plus the sum edgeMetric for all edges inside and on the boundary of the triangulation. Where triangleMetric is proportional to triangle aspect ratio edgeMetric is proportional to ( 1 - dihedralAngleCos )

briefdescription.para: Simple metric minimizing the sum of all edge lengths.

detaileddescription.para: Forbids connecting vertices from the same hole Simple metric minimizing edge length
Forbids connecting vertices from the same hole penalize for large area and face normal deviation from upDir All new faces should be parallel to given direction
Forbids connecting vertices from the same hole penalize for long edges and its deviation from upDir All new faces should be parallel to given direction

This metric minimizes the sum of triangleMetric for all triangles in the triangulation plus the sum edgeMetric for all edges inside and on the boundary of the triangulation. Where triangleMetric is proportional to weighted triangle area and triangle aspect ratio edgeMetric grows with angle between triangles as ( ( 1 - cos( x ) ) / ( 1 + cos( x ) ) ) ^ 4.

briefdescription.para: This metric minimizes summary projection of new edges to plane normal, (try do produce edges parallel to plane)

detaileddescription.para: This metric minimizes the maximal dihedral angle between the faces in the triangulation and on its boundary

This metric consists of two parts 1) for each triangle: it is the circumcircle diameter, this avoids the appearance of degenerate triangles; 2) for each edge: square root of double total area of triangles to its left and right times the factor depending extensionally on absolute dihedral angle between left and right triangles, this makes visually triangulated surface as smooth as possible. For planar holes it is the same as getCircumscribedMetric.

briefdescription.para: This metric maximizes the minimal angle among all faces in the triangulation.

detaileddescription.para: This metric is for triangulation construction with minimal summed area of triangles. Warning: this metric can produce degenerated triangles

briefdescription.para: This chapter represents documentation about hole triangulations or stitching two holes.
